label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me think about this. The question is asking whether following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward, take 8 steps right, then take 8 steps left. The options are yes or no. First, I need to visualize this. If I'm always facing forward, then "right" and "left" would be relative to my current direction. Wait, but if I take 8 steps to the right and then 8 steps to the left, would that cancel each other out? Let me think of a coordinate system. Let's say I start at (0,0) facing along the positive y-axis (up). My right direction would be along the positive x-axis (to the right), and my left direction would be along the negative x-axis (to the left). So taking 8 steps right would move me from (0,0) to (8,0). Then taking 8 steps left would move me back from (8,0) to (0,0). So in this case, I would end up back at the starting point. Wait, that seems straightforward. But maybe I need to consider if the direction changes during the steps? The instruction says "always face forward," which suggests that I don't change direction
